volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 1.2.153 - Section 1.3.215b)

sections.label: Section 1.2.153
sections.original: Age sane igitur , quando aequom oras , quam mox incendo rogum ?
sections.translation: Well , come then , since you request what ’ s fair , how soon am I to set fire to the pile ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.154_155
sections.original: dies quidem iam ad umbilicum est dimidiatus mortuos .
sections.translation: Why really , the day ’ s half dead already down to its navel [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.156
sections.original: Te morare , mihi quom obloquere .
sections.translation: While you are interrupting me , you are delaying yourself .

sections.label: Section 1.2.156b
sections.original: Oculum ecfodito per solum
sections.translation: Knock out my only eye [*] , Menaechmus , if I speak one word but what you bid me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.158
sections.original: Concede huc a foribus .
sections.translation: Come this way from the door .

sections.label: Section 1.2.158b
sections.original: Fiat .
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 1.2.158c
sections.original: Etiam concede huc .
sections.translation: Come still more this way .

sections.label: Section 1.2.158d
sections.original: Licet .
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 1.2.159
sections.original: Etiam nunc concede audacter ab leonino cavo .
sections.translation: Even still , step aside boldly from the lioness ’ s den .

sections.label: Section 1.2.160
sections.original: Eu edepol ne tu , ut ego opinor , esses agitator probus .
sections.translation: Well done ; by my troth , as I fancy , you really would bean excellent charioteers [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.161
sections.original: Quidum ?
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.161b
sections.original: Ne te uxor sequatur , respectas identidem .
sections.translation: That your wife mayn ’ t follow you , you are looking back ever and anon .

sections.label: Section 1.2.162
sections.original: Sed quid ais ?
sections.translation: But what say you ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.162b
sections.original: Egone ? id enim quod tu vis , id aio atque id nego .
sections.translation: What , I ? Why , whatever you choose , that same do I say , and that same do I deny .

sections.label: Section 1.2.163
sections.original: Ecquid tu de odore possis , si quid forte olfeceris ,
sections.translation: Could you make any conjecture at all from the smell , if perchance you were to take a smell at something ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.165
sections.original: ⋯ captum sit collegium .
sections.translation: Were the college of Augurs summoned ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.2.166
sections.original: Agedum odorare hanc quam ego habeo pallam . quid olet ? apstines ?
sections.translation: Come then , take a sniff at this mantle that I ’ m holding . What does it smell of ? Do you decline ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.167
sections.original: Summum ólfactare oportet vestimentum muliebre ,
sections.translation: It ’ s as well to smell the top of a woman ’ s garment ; for at this other place the nose is offended with an odour that can ’ t be washed out .

sections.label: Section 1.2.169
sections.original: Olfacta igitur hinc , Penicule . lepide ut fastidis .
sections.translation: Take a smell here then , Peniculus , as you are so daintily nice .

sections.label: Section 1.2.169b
sections.original: Decet .
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 1.2.170
sections.original: Quid igitur ? quid olet ? responde .
sections.translation: How now ? What does it smell of ? Answer me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.170b
sections.original: Furtum , scortum , prandium .
sections.translation: Theft , a mistress , and a breakfast . To you ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.2.172
sections.original: elocutus , nam
sections.translation: You have spoken out ⋯ now it shall be taken to this mistress of mine , the Courtesan Erotium . I ’ ll order a breakfast at once to be got ready , for me , you , and her ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: Inde usque ad diurnam stellam crastinam potabimus .
sections.translation: then will we booze away even to the morrow ’ s morning star .

sections.label: Section 1.2.175b
sections.original: Eu ,
sections.translation: Capital . You ’ ve spoken out distinctly . Am I to knock at the door then ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.176b
sections.original: Feri .
sections.translation: Knock — or hold , rather .

sections.label: Section 1.2.177b
sections.original: Mille passum commoratu'scommoratus es cantharum .
sections.translation: You ’ ve removed [*] the goblet a full mile by that .

sections.label: Section 1.2.178
sections.original: Placide pulta .
sections.translation: Knock gently .

sections.label: Section 1.2.178b
sections.original: Metuis , credo , ne fores Samiae sient .
sections.translation: You ’ re afraid , I think , that the doors are made of Samian crockery . ( Goes to knock .)

sections.label: Section 1.2.179_180
sections.original: Mane , mane obsecro hercle : eapse eccam exit . oh , solem vides
sections.translation: Hold , prithee , hold , i ’ faith ; see , she ’ s coming out herself . ( The door of EROTIUM ’ S house is opened .) Ha you behold the sun , is it not quite darkened in comparison with the bright rays of her person .

sections.label: Section 1.3.182
sections.original: Anime mi , Menaechme , salve .
sections.translation: My life , Menaechmus , save you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.182b
sections.original: Quid ego ?
sections.translation: And what for me ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.182c
sections.original: Extra numerum es mihi .
sections.translation: You are out of my number .

sections.label: Section 1.3.183
sections.original: Idem istuc aliis adscriptivis fieri ad legionem solet .
sections.translation: ⋯ that same thing is wont to be done for the other supernumeraries [*] of the legion .

sections.label: Section 1.3.184_185
sections.original: Ego istic mihi hodie adparari iussi apud te proelium .
sections.translation: I would order a skirmish to be got ready there at your house for me to-day .

sections.label: Section 1.3.186
sections.original: Hodie id fiet .
sections.translation: To-day it shall be done .

sections.label: Section 1.3.186b
sections.original: In eo uterque proelio potabimus ;
sections.translation: In that skirmish we two shall drink . Him shall you choose that shall be found there the better warrior with the goblet ; do you make up your mind with which of the two you ’ ll pass this night . How much , my love , when I look upon you , do I hate my wife .

sections.label: Section 1.3.190
sections.original: Interim nequis quin eius aliquid indutus sies .
sections.translation: Meantime , however , you cannot help being wrapped in something of hers . What ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.191b
sections.original: Induviae tuae atque uxoris exuviae , rosa .
sections.translation: ’ Tis a new dress for you , and a spoil from [*] my wife , my rosebud .

sections.label: Section 1.3.192
sections.original: Superas facile , ut superior sis mihi quam quisquam qui impetrant .
sections.translation: You have a ready way of prevailing , so as to be superior in my eyes to any one of those that pay me suit .

sections.label: Section 1.3.193
sections.original: Meretrix tantisper blanditur , dum illud quod rapiat videt ;
sections.translation: The harlot ’ s coaxing in the meantime , while she ’ s looking out what to plunder ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.3.194_195
sections.original: nam si amabas , iám oportebat nasum abreptum mordicus .
sections.translation: ( to EROTIUM ) for if you really loved him , by this his nose ought to have been off with your teething him . [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.196
sections.original: Sustine hoc , Penicule : exuvias facere quas vovi volo .
sections.translation: Take hold of this , Peniculus : I wish to dedicate the spoil that I ’ ve vowed .

sections.label: Section 1.3.197
sections.original: Cedo ; sed obsecro hercle , salta sic cum palla postea .
sections.translation: Give it me . ( Holds it while MENAECHMUS puts it on .) But , i ’ faith , prithee , do dance afterwards with the mantle on in this way .

sections.label: Section 1.3.198
sections.original: Ego saltabo ? sanus hercle non es .
sections.translation: I — dance ? I ’ faith , you ’ re not in your senses .

sections.label: Section 1.3.198b
sections.original: Egone an tu magis ?
sections.translation: Are you or I the most ? If you won ’ t dance , then take it off .

sections.label: Section 1.3.199b
sections.original: Nimio ego hanc periculo
sections.translation: At a great risk have I

sections.label: Section 1.3.200
sections.original: surrupui hodie . meo quidem animo ab Hippolyta subcingulum
sections.translation: stolen this to-day . In my opinion , indeed , Hercules didn ’ t ever carry off the belt from Hippolyta [*] with danger as great . Take this for yourself ( he takes it off , and gives her the mantle ) , since you are the only one alive that ’ s compliant with my humours .

sections.label: Section 1.3.203
sections.original: Hoc animo decet animatos esse amatores probos .
sections.translation: With such feelings ’ tis proper that real lovers should be animated .

sections.label: Section 1.3.204
sections.original: Qui quidem ad mendicitatem se properent detrudere .
sections.translation: Those , indeed , who are making haste to bring themselves down to beggary .

sections.label: Section 1.3.205
sections.original: Quattuor minis ego emi istanc anno uxori meae .
sections.translation: I purchased that for my wife a year since at the price of four minae .

sections.label: Section 1.3.206
sections.original: Quattuor minae perierunt plane , ut ratio redditur .
sections.translation: The four minae are clearly gone for ever , as the account now stands .

sections.label: Section 1.3.207
sections.original: Scin quid volo ego te accurare ?
sections.translation: Do you know what I wish you to attend to ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.207b
sections.original: Scio , curabo quae voles .
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; but I ’ ll attend to whatever you do wish .

sections.label: Section 1.3.208
sections.original: Iube igitur tribus nóbis apud te prandium accurarier
sections.translation: Order a breakfast , then , to be provided for us three at your house , and some dainties to be purchased at the market ;

sections.label: Section 1.3.210
sections.original: glandionidam suillam , laridum pernonidam ,
sections.translation: kernels of boars ’ neck , or bacon off the gammon [*] , or pig ’ s head , or something in that way , which , when cooked and placed on table before me , may promote an appetite like a kite ’ s : and-forthwith —

sections.label: Section 1.3.213b
sections.original: Licet ecastor .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I will .

sections.label: Section 1.3.213c
sections.original: Nos prodimus ad forum .
sections.translation: We ’ re going to the Forum : we shall be here just now . While it ’ s cooking , we ’ ll take a whet in the meantime .

sections.label: Section 1.3.215
sections.original: Quando vis veni , parata res erit .
sections.translation: Come when you like , the things shall be ready .

sections.label: Section 1.3.215b
sections.original: Propera modo .
sections.translation: Only make haste , then . Do you follow me
